title: Easy Comforting Algerian Tofu Tagine
description: A sour and fluffy Algerian dish featuring marinated tofu, this tagine is a comforting and low-calorie-friendly option for a weeknight dinner. It combines the flavors of Africa and the Middle East in a single pot.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed tofu and cook until it is lightly browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the tagine spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lemon juice, chicken broth, and dried prunes, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the tagine or Dutch oven, and simmer the mixture for 20 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot over couscous or rice.
